items.title: Microsoft: Some Teams users can’t join meetings after Edge update
items.summary: Microsoft confirmed that a recent Microsoft Edge browser update introduced a bug that prevents Windows users from joining Teams meetings. [...]

items.title: UK warns of Chinese hackers using proxy networks to evade detection
items.summary: The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-UK) and international partners warned that China-nexus hackers are increasingly using large-scale proxy networks of hijacked consumer devices to evade detection and disguise their…

items.title: New GopherWhisper APT group abuses Outlook, Slack, Discord for comms
items.summary: A previously undocumented state-backed threat actor named GopherWhisper is using a Go-based custom toolkit and legitimate services like Microsoft 365 Outlook, Slack, and Discord in attacks against government entities. [...]

items.title: [Webinar] Mythos Reality Check: Beating Automated Exploitation at AI Speed
items.summary: Imagine a world where hackers don't sleep, don't take breaks, and find weak spots in your systems instantly. Well, that world is already here. Thanks to AI, attackers are now launching automated, large-scale exploits faster than ever…

items.title: Project Glasswing Proved AI Can Find the Bugs. Who's Going to Fix Them?
items.summary: Last week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, an AI model so effective at discovering software vulnerabilities that they took the extraordinary step of postponing its public release. Instead, the company has given access to Apple,…

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch BlueHammer flaw exploited as zero-day
items.summary: CISA has ordered U.S. federal agencies to patch a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation flaw (dubbed BlueHammer) that has been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: Apple Patches Exploited Notification Flaw, (Thu, Apr 23rd)
items.summary: Apple yesterday released iOS/iPadOS 26.4.2 and iOS/iPadOS 18.7.8. This update fixes a single Notification Services vulnerability, CVE-2026-28950:

items.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
items.summary: The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.

items.title: China-Linked GopherWhisper Infects 12 Mongolian Government Systems with Go Backdoors
items.summary: Mongolian governmental institutions have emerged as the target of a previously undocumented China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group tracked as GopherWhisper. "The group wields a wide array of tools mostly written in Go, using…

items.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
items.summary: Vercel on Wednesday revealed that it has identified an additional set of customer accounts that were compromised as part of a security incident that enabled unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company said it made the…

items.title: Apple Fixes iOS Flaw That Let FBI Recover Deleted Signal Messages
items.summary: Apple has rolled out a software fix for iOS and iPadOS to address a Notification Services flaw that stored notifications marked for deletion on the device. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28950 (CVSS score: N/A), has been described…

items.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
items.summary: The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.

items.summary: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]

items.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
items.summary: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

items.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
items.summary: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]

items.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
items.summary: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]

items.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown threat actors…

items.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both…

items.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
items.summary: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook…

items.title: Spain dismantles major $4.7M manga piracy platform, arrests four
items.summary: The Spanish police have dismantled the largest Spanish-language manga piracy platform, operating since 2014, with millions of monthly users from around the globe. [...]

items.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
items.summary: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

items.title: Electricity Is a Growing Area of Cyber Risk
items.summary: IT has long been concerned about ensuring systems receive the right amount of electricity. Cyberattackers are realizing they can manipulate voltage fluctuations for their purposes, too.

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.summary: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
items.summary: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

items.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
items.summary: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.summary: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: Multiples vulnérabilités dans le noyau Linux de SUSE (17 avril 2026)
items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans le noyau Linux de SUSE. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer un problème de sécurité non spécifié par l'éditeur.

items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans IBM QRadar. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer un déni de service à distance.

items.title: Multiples vulnérabilités dans les produits Cisco (16 avril 2026)
items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans les produits Cisco. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer une exécution de code arbitraire à distance et un contournement de la politique de sécurité.
